growth_records.medium: Pro99 (Sargasso seawater base, Moore et al. recipe)
growth_records.cite: Zhu et al. Characterization of phytoplankton-excreted metabolites mediating carbon flux through the surface ocean. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2026). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2531765123. [PMC13012070]
growth_records.snippet: Prochlorococcus marinus MIT9301 ... Pro99 ... 24 ... 90 (13:11) ... Exponential phase growth rate (d-1) 0.52
growth_records.notes: Table 1 reports exponential-phase growth rate = 0.52 d-1 directly, calculated by the authors from daily cell-count data (axenic culture in 35 mL Pro99 medium, 13:11 h light:dark, 90 umol photons m-2 s-1). Converted to per-hour: mu = 0.52/24 = 0.02167 h-1; doubling_time_h = ln2/0.02167 = 31.99 h.

growth_records.cite: Steglich et al. Short RNA half-lives in the slow-growing marine cyanobacterium Prochlorococcus. Genome Biology (2010). DOI:10.1186/gb-2010-11-5-r54. [PMC2897979]
growth_records.snippet: Prochlorococcus MED4 was grown at 21 C in Sargasso seawater-based Pro99 medium under 30 umol quanta m-2 s-1 continuous cool white light with a growth rate of 0.325 day-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate directly reported in Materials and Methods as 0.325 day^-1 for photoautotrophic batch culture under continuous light (30 umol quanta m-2 s-1); converted to per-hour units: 0.325/24 = 0.01354 h^-1. Doubling_time_h calculated as ln2/mu = 0.693/0.01354 = 51.2 h (consistent with the paper's separate statement of a 'generation time of over 24 hours' for Prochlorococcus). Oxygen field set to aerobic as a phototrophic aerobic culture (oxygenic photosynthesis); this is not a heterotrophic aerobic respiration context. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates reported; light intensity (30 umol quanta m-2 s-1) is the limiting input but is not a chemical substrate uptake rate as defined by this schema.

growth_records.cite: Read et al. Nitrogen cost minimization is promoted by structural changes in the transcriptome of N-deprived Prochlorococcus cells. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.88. [PMC5607370]
growth_records.snippet: For these batch source cultures, fluorescence-based measurements yielded a growth rate of 0.60 d-1 +/-0.01 (1 standard deviation)
growth_records.notes: Growth rate reported directly in paper as 0.60 d-1 +/- 0.01 (fluorescence-based, log-linear portion of bulk culture fluorescence growth curve). Converted to per-hour: 0.60/24 = 0.025 h-1. Doubling time calculated as ln(2)/mu = ln(2)/0.60 d-1 = 1.155 d = 27.7 h. Cells acclimated by ~20 generations of serial transfer in mid-exponential phase before measurement; triplicate 4.5 L batch cultures, 25 C, continuous illumination ~55 umol photons m-2 s-1, Pro99 medium with 800 uM NH4Cl as sole assimilable N source.

growth_records.cite: Read et al. Nitrogen cost minimization is promoted by structural changes in the transcriptome of N-deprived Prochlorococcus cells. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.88. [PMC5607370]
growth_records.snippet: flow cytometry-based measurements yielded a growth rate of 0.67 d-1 +/-0.14 (1 standard deviation)
growth_records.notes: Same batch source cultures as the fluorescence-based estimate but measured by flow-cytometry cell counts, reported directly as 0.67 d-1 +/- 0.14. Converted to per-hour: 0.67/24 = 0.0279 h-1. Doubling time calculated as ln(2)/mu = ln(2)/0.67 d-1 = 1.034 d = 24.8 h.

growth_records.strain: MED4 (ecotype HLI; synonymous genotypes PCC9511, CCMP1378, CCMP1986)

up.method: short-term [14C]bicarbonate primary production (net and gross primary production); values plotted in Fig. 1 over the diel cycle but no single numeric rate given in the main text

yields.elemental_composition: Particulate C/P molar ratio = 121:1 +/- 17:1 under balanced growth (molar NH4+/H2PO4- = 16:1); rose to 464:1 +/- 28:1 under P-limitation (molar NH4+/H2PO4- = 800:1). Cited to reference 14 (Bertilsson et al.), not measured in this study.

growth_records.cite: Casey, John R. et al. Adaptive Evolution of Phosphorus Metabolism in Prochlorococcus. mSystems (2016). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00065-16. [PMC5111396]
growth_records.snippet: By simulating their growth conditions over a diel cycle, we calculated an optimal growth rate of 0.62 day-1, while the experimental growth rates were 0.62 +/- 0.04 day-1 (mean +/- standard deviation). ... 14-h/10-h light/dark cycle at 20 to
growth_records.notes: EXPERIMENTAL growth rate = 0.62 +/- 0.04 day-1 for P. marinus MED4 on PRO99, 14h/10h light/dark, 20-24 C. This value is CITED FROM Zinser et al. (reference 26 in this paper; the most comprehensive data set relating carbon fixation and photophysiology to growth), and the iJC568 model reproduced it (computed 0.62 day-1). Conversions: mu = 0.62 day-1 / 24 h/day = 0.02583 h-1 (rounded 0.0258). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.62 = 1.118 day = 1.118 x 24 = 26.8 h. This study's own axenic MED4 culture was grown at 24 C, peak irradiance 45 umol photons m-2 s-1 (Materials and Methods); P-limited variant used 2 uM H2PO4- giving N/P ratio 200, but no growth rate was reported for the P-limited condition so it is not emitted as a separate record. Organism is an obligate photoautotroph, so there is no organic substrate uptake rate; the only measured flux is [14C]bicarbonate primary production, reported graphically (Fig. 1) with no single numeric value in text. iJC568 is a constraint-based FBA reconstruction (not a 13C-MFA study), so internal fluxes are model-computed, not experimental; mfa_fluxes left empty. Full PRO99 composition not given in this paper (cited to ref 31).
